Title: P1_ValidateEnvironmentVariablesCompleteness
Step Action: Open the Environment Variables spreadsheet and navigate to the client-specific tab
Step Expected Result: Client tab exists and is accessible in SharePoint

Step Action: Confirm all rows where Input Type = Manual in Column F have a value entered in Column C
Step Expected Result: No blank cells exist for any Manual input row

Step Action: Confirm Fixed fields (Environment
Step Expected Result: Environment Prefix
column4: Server Number) have not been modified
column5: Fixed fields retain their pre-configured values and formulas are intact

Step Action: Confirm Auto-Generated fields (AWS Account Name
Step Expected Result: Server Names) have calculated correctly from Manual inputs
column4: Auto-Generated fields display values derived from the entered Manual inputs with no formula errors

Title: P1_ValidateTierCalculation
Step Action: Open the Environment Variables spreadsheet and navigate to the client-specific tab
Step Expected Result: Client tab is accessible

Step Action: Locate the Client Member Count value in Column C
Step Expected Result: Member count value is present and numeric

Step Action: Verify the Tier value in Column C matches the correct threshold: Tier 1 if >= 1000000 / Tier 2 if >= 100000 / Tier 3 if < 100000
Step Expected Result: Tier value matches the expected threshold based on the member count entered

Title: P1_ValidateStakeholderDataAccuracy
Step Action: Collect the signed-off values from stakeholders for Tenant Name / Tenant ID / Tenant Country Code / Member Count
Step Expected Result: Stakeholder confirmation is documented

Step Action: Compare each stakeholder-provided value against the corresponding entry in Column C of the Environment Variables spreadsheet
Step Expected Result: All Manual fields match the stakeholder-provided values exactly with no discrepancies

Step Action: Confirm Tenant ID is unique by checking it does not already exist in the Master_Sheet or other client tabs
Step Expected Result: Tenant ID is not duplicated across any existing tab in the workbook

Title: P2_ValidatePhase2SpreadsheetFields
Step Action: Open the Environment Variables spreadsheet and navigate to the client-specific tab
Step Expected Result: Client tab is accessible

Step Action: Confirm all Phase 2 Manual fields are populated in Column C: Cost Center / Primary Owner / Secondary Owner / Support Group / Application / ATG / New or Existing Role
Step Expected Result: No blank cells exist for any Phase 2 Manual field

Step Action: Confirm the Cost Center value is a 6-digit numeric code
Step Expected Result: Cost Center field contains exactly 6 digits

Step Action: Confirm Primary Owner is a valid email address and Secondary Owner is a distribution list email
Step Expected Result: Both email formats are valid and match stakeholder-provided values

Title: P2_ValidateServiceNowAWSAccountTicket
Step Action: Log into DQ Service Now using Okta Tile
Step Expected Result: ServiceNow is accessible and the home page loads

Step Action: Navigate to Service Catalog and locate the submitted AWS Accounts Request ticket
Step Expected Result: Ticket is present with status visible

Step Action: Verify ticket Type of Request = Cloud Request and Cloud Provider = AWS
Step Expected Result: Both fields match the expected values

Step Action: Verify the Additional Comments table in the ticket contains the correct AWS Accounts, Cost Center, Environment, Environment Prefix, Primary Owner, Secondary Owner, Support Group, Application, ATG, and Role values
Step Expected Result: All values in the ticket match the corresponding entries in the Environment Variables spreadsheet with no discrepancies

Step Action: Confirm the ticket has been assigned to the Cloud Infrastructure Team
Step Expected Result: Ticket assignee is Erik Rodriguez or the Cloud Infrastructure Team group

Title: P2_ValidateAWSAccountsCreated
Step Action: Confirm that Cloud Infrastructure Team has provided the new AWS account details
Step Expected Result: Account name and ID details received from Cloud Infrastructure Team

Step Action: Log into AWS via Okta SSO and verify the new accounts are visible (PROD, QAR, DEV)
Step Expected Result: All three environment accounts appear in the AWS account list

Step Action: Confirm account names follow the Auto-Generated naming convention from the Environment Variables spreadsheet (e.g. Dental-SLE-PROD-us-east-1)
Step Expected Result: Each account name matches exactly what is listed in the spreadsheet

Title: P2_1_ValidateAWSIAMRoles
Step Action: Log into AWS via Okta SSO and switch to the tenant DEV account
Step Expected Result: DEV account is accessible without error

Step Action: Navigate to IAM and confirm the expected roles exist (existing role base such as aws-oktaSSO or new custom roles as per Environment Variables)
Step Expected Result: All required IAM roles are present in the account

Step Action: Repeat role verification for QAR and PROD accounts
Step Expected Result: IAM roles exist and are correctly configured in all three environments

Title: P2_1_ValidateAWSOktaSSOAccess
Step Action: Using an authorized team member account log into AWS DEV via the Okta Tile
Step Expected Result: Login succeeds and the AWS console is accessible for the DEV account

Step Action: Switch to the appropriate tenant-specific IAM role within the DEV account
Step Expected Result: Role switch succeeds and the correct account ID is shown in the console header

Step Action: Repeat login and role switch for QAR and PROD accounts
Step Expected Result: All three environments are accessible using the assigned IAM roles

Title: P3_ValidateJiraInfraProvisioningTicket
Step Action: Log into Jira and navigate to the client-specific backlog
Step Expected Result: Backlog is accessible

Step Action: Locate the Tenant AWS Provisioning user story for this tenant
Step Expected Result: User story exists with title matching Tenant AWS Provisioning - [Tenant Name]

Step Action: Verify the description contains links to the Azure DevOps architecture folder and the Environment Variables spreadsheet
Step Expected Result: Both links are present and navigate to the correct resources

Step Action: Verify Acceptance Criteria is set to: AWS cloud server infrastructure setup should be complete and infrastructure validated for the Tenant
Step Expected Result: Acceptance criteria text matches exactly

Step Action: Confirm the ticket is assigned to the Cloud Infrastructure Team and status is Done or has a completion comment
Step Expected Result: Ticket indicates provisioning is complete

Title: P3_ValidateEC2ServersProvisioned
Step Action: Log into AWS via Okta Tile and switch to the DEV environment role
Step Expected Result: AWS console is accessible for the DEV account

Step Action: Navigate to EC2 and click Instances (running)
Step Expected Result: EC2 instances list is displayed

Step Action: Compare every running instance name against the expected server list in the Environment Variables spreadsheet for DEV
Step Expected Result: All expected servers from the spreadsheet are present in the running instances list with no missing servers

Step Action: Verify each instance Status Check shows 2/2 checks passed (System Status Check and Instance Status Check)
Step Expected Result: All instances show passing status checks with no failed checks

Step Action: Repeat steps for QAR and PROD environments as each becomes available
Step Expected Result: All expected servers are running and passing status checks in each environment

Step Action: Confirm the ticket has been completed and closed
Step Expected Result: Ticket status is Closed or Resolved

Step Action: Resolve the Payment Web and Utility App ALB aliases
Step Expected Result: Both aliases resolve to the correct endpoints with no DNS errors

column5: and the domain name matches the expected tenant domain

Title: P3_3_ValidateInfoSecControls

Title: P4_ValidateDatabaseRestoration

Step Expected Result: All required databases (Core DB
column7: etc.) are listed on the server

Step Expected Result: Storage capacity matches the Tier specification with no under-provisioning

Step Action: Confirm DBA team has reviewed and approved the sizing

Step Expected Result: All environment database servers match the Tier specification

Title: P5_ValidateApplicationURLAccessibility_Playwright
Step Action: Confirm the Playwright test suite is configured with the correct DEV base URL from the Environment Variables spreadsheet
Step Expected Result: Playwright environment file reflects the correct tenant DEV URL with no placeholder values

Step Action: Execute the existing Playwright smoke test suite targeting the DEV environment
Step Expected Result: Playwright run completes with all tests passing and no failures reported

Step Action: Review the Playwright test report and confirm URL accessibility tests pass for Windward Web / Payment Web / Configuration Web
Step Expected Result: All URL navigation tests show Pass status in the Playwright report

Step Action: Raise a Jira defect for any Playwright test that fails and block phase exit until resolved
Step Expected Result: All failures are logged in Jira with Blocker severity and assigned to the Application Services Team

Step Action: Reconfigure Playwright with the QAR base URL and repeat execution; then repeat for PROD
Step Expected Result: Playwright suite passes for QAR and PROD with no failures

Step Expected Result: All health endpoint tests in the Playwright report show Pass with HTTP 200 responses

Step Action: Review Playwright output for any health endpoint returning an unexpected status or error
Step Expected Result: No health endpoint test failures exist in the report

Step Action: Reconfigure for QAR and PROD and re-execute; confirm all health tests pass
Step Expected Result: Health endpoint tests pass across all three environments
